name: growth-chart
title: Growth Chart
description: A child's growth against a named percentile reference, with corrected age stated rather than assumed.

props.name: data
props.description: `{ x, y, percentile?, date? }` — x is age in `xUnit`, corrected if `correctedAge`.

props.name: percentiles
props.description: Required. The reference's own published curves — never bundled with the component.

props.name: reference
props.description: Required. Which reference and for whom: "WHO 0–2 years, girls". Rendered on the chart.

props.name: correctedAge / gestationAtBirth
props.description: Stated, never inferred. Shown as a badge on the chart.

clinicalNotes: `reference` and `percentiles` are both required and neither ships with the component: the same weight lands on a different percentile against WHO, CDC, or a local standard, so a growth chart that quietly chose one would be putting a clinical claim in the caller's mouth. The reference is named on the chart itself for the same reason. `correctedAge` is stated explicitly because plotting a preterm infant at chronological age drags them down the centiles and invents a growth problem that does not exist — and because the correction eventually stops applying, which is a clinical decision, not a UI one. The component plots what it is given and computes no percentile. A single point says very little; the shape of the line is the reading, and the caption says so.
notes: The flagship of the Clinical Data round. Percentile curves are drawn recessive (dashed, muted); the child's series is the only prominent mark. Composes chart + badge.
